title: Signal Alignment Theory: Application Study: Economic Signals Across 1925 to 2025: EDI 01 - 100: Expanded Audit Trail
author: Tanner, Christopher
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Abstract:  This study presents an LLM-assisted diagnostic implementation of Signal Alignment Theory (SAT) applied to the Economic Dynamics Index (EDI), a structured dataset of 100 temporally distributed macroeconomic observations. A calibrated large language model (Claude, January 2026 configuration) was pre-conditioned on SAT theoretical foundations and used to execute a constrained Q12–Q14 diagnostic funnel producing six-dimensional energy vectors, directional field estimates, amplitude positions, and phase-state classifications. Each EDI entry was expanded into a three-state temporal window (Pₜ₋₁, Pₜ, Pₜ₊₁), yielding 300 phase-state classifications and 200 empirically derived transitions. A secondary LLM-based audit layer (ChatGPT) enforced consistency constraints through a binary validation protocol, ensuring internal coherence of energy–phase mappings and transition logic without introducing new theoretical constructs. The resulting dataset supports reproducible extraction of phase frequencies, transition probabilities, and canonical sequence conformance metrics under a structurally constrained and auditable dual-model architecture. This operational framework formalizes LLM-assisted macroeconomic phase diagnostics as a controlled, verifiable procedure rather than a generative inference system.

A secondary LLM-based audit layer (ChatGPT) enforced consistency constraints through a binary validation protocol, ensuring internal coherence of energy–phase mappings and transition logic without introducing new theoretical constructs.
